reviews.review_title: One of the best on the quest!!
reviews.review_description:
This game is outstanding, the graphics, story, mechanics, difficulty levels, are all spot on.  The eerie feel and not knowing which monsters going to attack and when, really do put you right on the edge, easily one of the scariest games on the quest.  My only gripe is that I completed it too quickly, although the price reflects the length of the game.  I normally steer clear of games that are too cheap because they tend to disappoint, however this game breaks that paradigm.

Well done to the developers, now when are you going to make the sequel so we can rescue Ashley !!!

This game is one of the most suspenseful I have encountered.  It will make you jump as you wind your way among the unfortunate dead.   My advice:  stay near a save station so you don’t need to retread the same ground.  Well worth the money.

This game is good so far until I got stuck on a certain part of the game and cannot move forward from. I thought perhaps I am the problem, but no. It’s the game glitching. There is a puzzle about getting the code to the secure room of the penthouse of the owner of the hotel. I solved the puzzle. But the number is wrong. So I thought I did it wrong. But even as I looked online, the answer was correct that I found. Yet the it kept saying it’s wrong. Now I’m not sure what to do and how to proceed or where to complain about this to begin with. Hopefully this would help?
This game forces you into stressful and quick thinking situations really cleverly, with the perfect environment and really well written story. I want updates yesterday… we gotta save Ashley!
Brilliant 👍😊
This is one of the best games I have ever played, thanks to its highly immersive VR experience. The storytelling, the graphics,everything about this game impresses me in every way

PPH is an awesome game, but it just kinda feels like a demo if you know what I mean. I love this game, it’s great but once you beat it, it just feels like you’re left on a cliffhanger. I hope to see this game fully completed in the future. Or if a DLC gets added, i will most definitely buy it.

This is definitely one of the highest-quality games on Quest - a mature, modern take on the early Resident Evil titles. It features excellent graphics (with dynamic shadows!), solid level design, plenty of variety, and above all, meticulous attention to detail. The whole experience feels cohesive, engaging, eerie, and real. Highly recommended, but keep in mind that the full playthrough lasts only about 4–5 hours.
NEED more

reviews.review_title: Probably my favorite VR horror now
reviews.review_description: Basically old resident evil in VR with really good graphics. The only issues I have are 1. it's a bit short, but it fits the price (on sale it's basically a steal) though I was really impressed by voice acting, overall story, exploring, puzzles and gunplay. 2. Something I was missing a little bit was maybe melee combat like in S&S or more guns (there are only 2, but they feel rly good), yet it does not ruin the experience and I hope we'll get part two soon

Great atmosphere and story which really built up the tension. However, you’re soon without bullets - which are the only means of combat. By the time you have the techniques down, it’s too late without a full restart.  Meaning, it gets tedious quickly. 
Wouldn’t recommend.
Zombies are scary looks great on quest 3 gunplay is awesome ui is easy to navigate easy to grasp great mechanics and tools bro genuinely great game please release part 2 take my money
Such an eerie and uneasy feeling to the game. Great story. Hope they make a second game

You end up wandering around an old hotel shooting zombies. If you run out of batteries, you're wandering around in the dark. So make sure you find all the batteries You can, otherwise the game is pointless. As soon as you solve a puzzle and move forward, you're immediately stopped again. Pretty frustrating.
I hope the devs make part 2 I know it’s been awhile but I’m praying everyday they make another one
2 hours trying to get the be haptics working because i love this game there's nothing in the end game settings there's nothing this is a scary joke a moderately speechless

This game is great but is unfinished for sure. I assumed the comments were exaggerating but this is true. 
The graphics, gameplay and atmosphere of the game are just stunning, but it abruptly ends on a cliffhanger after not even 2 hours of gameplay, and that's taking my time, opening every room and every drawer I could find. 
I would understand if the price was lower and labeled as part 1 but this is just ridiculous.

Wow just finished the game and wasn't happy with the way the game ended..there definitely should be a part 2..Like there's all those floors to explore yet the game ends with only 3 floors and no where near the roof to save the sister..I was very disappointed in how this game ended..please get a part 2 to finish all the floors and so we can  actually save the sister...other than that this game is amazing with jump scares and etc..just really wanted to get to the roof and explore more of the hotel instead of it just ending..

reviews.review_title: I LOVE THIS GAME SO MUCH
reviews.review_description: PLS MAKE A 2ND ONE I NEED TO KNOW HOW IT ENDS I BEG YOU THE STORY IS SO AMAZING

The best game I played on VR. It nails everything you want in a horror game. Great visuals, lots of blood, puzzles are great, action makes you feel like you’re in a movie and it scares the crap out of me. Please make more of these! I was looking for something similar to RE4 and this exceeded my expectations by a lot. Might be better than RE4. I heard it’s really short(I’m still not done with it) but it’s also very cheap for what you’re getting. Definitely high quality gaming.

reviews.review_title: Would be 5 stars if a bit longer
reviews.review_description: Great game, feels like RE. But short and ends abruptly. Would be 5 stars if a bit longer

This game is excellent. I haven't finished it yet, and I can see from other reviews that it ends with a cliffhanger. I really hope they will do a part 2 then, because this game is a must buy for everyone! It is really really good! The atmosphere, the mechanics the graphic... Everything is super!
Its the best horror game I play on quest

Great game but thr game is so dark i cant evenly tell where im heading, NOWHERE in the game settings is there eventually a option to put upp the brightness, next to it, there is an option to blink/teleport, we cant set that off either, your running away in a second you somehow missclick and you teleport, ive fallen 3 times through the map by mistake and then died...


The game is really good atmospheric wise and gameplay wise, but the game is not done and the devs seem to care in a different way then i normally expect to happen, 

Ive got this game for 6 euros on a discount and i will still refund it and never touch it again, or they should fix the game, cause i cant see where im going i died 3 times in the first hour already because i ddi not see the zombies while on the steam version i can see them...?

the game was really fun and really good graphics  but no matter how long i wait the game won’t turn on and also the battery’s in the flash light die so fast and then im wondering in the dark trying to find battery’s

This is one of the best single player campaign experiences you can get in VR developed by an indie studio. The horror, the gun play, the puzzles, gorgeous presentation, RE1 quality voice acting, it all comes together for a superb experience. The one major complaint people will have is that it ends way too soon, but what is there is top notch. 

Starting with horror, I jumped several times throughout the game. Compared to it's closest competition on quest in Resident Evil 4, it's scarier and way more intense. The hunt for batteries while your light slowly drains as you traverse the dark hallways and air ducts of the hotel are unnerving. You'll be listening closely to make sure nothing sneaks up on you or jumps out at you and your main reprieve is that RE style save rooms where the music is calming. The enemy design is also very realistic, some of the best on quest for sure. 

Presentation is another huge win, the dark areas are a real highlight as well as the zombies themselves. They are much more intimidating and gory than Arizona Sunshine and The Walking Dead S&S. Character models are decent too though there are only a few of them that are not trying to eat you. My only real complaint is that zombies lack shadows except from your flashlight. However, that is mostly noticeable in the opening areas before you get the flashlight. Once you have that, your light projects real time shadows off of things like zombies, your weapon, and other items in the world in a very pleasing and believable way. 

I played on medium and found the playthrough to have a few challenging areas where I died multiple times. I almost never ran out of supplies. This was exactly what I wanted for a first playthrough and I could see myself playing it again on a harder difficulty. There was just enough stress to make things interesting but I rarely was frustrated to the point I wanted to quit. Guns felt good, exploration in this kind of game is rewarding and what you expect. You open drawers, cabinets, lockers, etc and look for supplies to keep your march through the hotel ongoing. The belt was a little unreliable compared to some other games and I did have to tweak the settings to align properly but it was nothing major. 

Unfortunately this game should be labeled "part 1" as you feel like the game ends with an entire act or more left on the table. The game doesn't have an ending really, it just ends! Please devs, make a sequel, part 2, epilogue, something! This is way too good not to have a proper ending! It deserves to be mentioned with other horror greats. I highly recommend it if you like Resident Evil or that style of game.

Absolutely loved the game. It’s the first game in a while that made me jump especially with my high powered headphones. However I was not expecting the game to come to in the end In the middle of the plot. I understand what the devs were trying to accomplish by leaving the cliffhanger. But devs, if your reading this, I think I speak for all who enjoy this game, GIVE US THE OTHER HALF OF THE GAME! We must see how it ends!

The game offers a good and immersive atmosphere with an interesting story. The graphics and the physics are well made and I like the fact that we can pick up quite a few objects (but not all).
However, the animations when being attacked need improvement and pushing away the enemies is quite clunky. The worst part, unfortunately, is the duration and the fact that we don't have the whole game which is weird, especially for the price. Play time is roughly 3 hours.

Suggestions for the devs: it could be better if we could drop the items from our inventory (they are ranged automatically) and if using an item like a key, instead of opening the inventory menu every time, just be able to grab it directly from the wrist.
I would like to see some melee combat and being able to use other types of weapons. 
3 bullets per zombie (they wake after a while) is a bit too much.

4.5 out of 5 from me. Let's get it out of the way, yes it's a 2-4 hour game. But it's also only 20 bucks and FANTASTIC. Plus, with collectibles and different difficulties, there's definitely replay value, as I'll be doing this spooky season! I've played a ton of horror and VR games and this is one of the most tense I've ever played. Multiple times I had that "I'm so screwed! I can't even go back out there, I'm screwed!" moment. Took me right back to playing the OG Resident Evil. The atmosphere, the sound and art design is obviously crucial for horror and this game 100% has the magic sauce. PLEASE be working on part 2 devs! And to all you horror fans, PLEASE buy this game, support these devs so we can (hopefully) get more! If you read this devs, first off, THANK YOU for an amazing experience! Second, as I'm sure you've heard, the in-game menu is a bit, meh. Except the map. That works great. If you allowed the player to auto grab the appropriate door key from our pouch when near the door, like with the other items, we'd only need to even open the menu...twice? Pulling the keys from the flat menu was slightly immersion breaking. More noticeable in a game dripping with immersive atmosphere such as this. Also I found the um, ending part to be a bit glitchy. Certain actions didn't seem to register as they should. Trying to avoid any spoilers. If you've played it, you'll know what I'm talking about. All in all, I LOVED this game!

The best zombie game on the quest period. Its short, but amazingly fun. Wish there would be a longer sequel. Maybe in the future as hardware advances, and more people cross over to VR. Thank you devs for all your hard work, and time on this game.
Great game, the story is  just a little bit to short

reviews.review_title: Liked the game, where is the rest?
reviews.review_description: I really enjoyed this game, but there is no ending. And weren't we told there would be another update or part 2 with the ending? I've heard nothing about it. It's been 3 years. I wrote this review 2 years ago. Still no response.

reviews.review_title: One of the best... but half a game?
reviews.review_description:
The game plays brilliantly 👏 
The shooting and reloading mechanics are up there with the best of them.
The graphics (played on meta 3) are some of the best, with great monster designs and plenty of gore. 
Most of all, it's actually scary, with great use of light and sound that build suspense.

But spoiler alert....


It's clearly not finished. The game ends half way through the plot/exploring the hotel. 

I want to rate this higher, and if this was advertised as "part 1" I may of done so, but it appears if its a full game on the store. 


If part 2 is ever released Devs, you have my money. It's far too good not to finish guys!

reviews.review_title: Do not sleep on this game!
reviews.review_description: All I have to say is I played this in little chunks just cause it’s scary and I don’t wanna undersell it. It is very good from the gameplay to the graphics to the mechanics.. I would definitely recommend this to anybody and I can’t wait to see what else these Devs pump out next!￼
I had the first propagation which was OK but this one is another level! Definitely a Must have in your library 💯

Since stepping into VR 2 years ago I always hoped capcom would remake resident evil 1 for VR but sadly it has never happened. This game is as close as you can get, the atmosphere is creepy as hell your not overwhelmed with zombies like other games and there are puzzles to solve to reach other areas. This is an amazing survival horror experience not very long but highly recommended. Comparing it to red evil is the highest praise anyone could give. Well done!

In my opinion, with the HD optimizer installed, this game rubs shoulders with Behemoth, Batman and Arizona Sunshine Remake. 
Red Matter 2 is still the best VR game of all time so it's not in that league. 
However...
For pure tension and fear it's unrivalled.  S***s all over Alien Incursion and Miranda. 
Only question is "When is the sequel coming out already?" It's been forever.

Ok, first up, it's a pretty good game...the graphics are adequate, certainly very good for the price, and the tension and atmosphere are primo!  The only thing that lets it down is that there does not appear to be a useable autosave...yes, you can save progress at occasional guestbook checkpoints, but this is not convenient, especially if you are snatching a few minutes of game time here and there and find you then need to redo a whole lot of progress which makes it extremely frustrating.  Seriously devs, this should be a no-brainer...just write the autosave file on exit, even if only on an elegant exit.
The atmosphere, the story, the mechanics, the immersion, everything!!!!! I NEED a sequel!!!!

I don't usually write reviews for games but I feel this one deserves it. Propagation: Paradise Hotel is one of if not the best VR horror experience I've ever been able to play! The graphics are brilliant and the creatures are horrific in a great way! The atmosphere of the hotel is amazing especially when paired with the sound design! You're constantly wondering what this sound was or what that one was or if that seemingly dead body just twitched. Some complain about the zombies being able to wake back up after shooting them but I believe it's a great way to make the player never feel truly safe. There's a constant sense of dread wherever you go and it's even worse when you're low on batteries or ammo. My only real complaints are that the dialogue sometimes feels unnatural or without personality and the game is just too short. Apart from those small things this game deserves a 5 star review and I will be recommending this to anyone looking for actually well made VR scares.
Man this is a wild experience fun but had me full of anxiety I’m a fan of horror but I had to take breaks was genuinely nerve wrecking we in the future and that’s all I can say!!!
Very fun game

Imagine that someone took Resident Evil 1 Remake from the Gamecube and made it into a VR game, then made the atmosphere a lot more scarier, made so the zombies never really died but would revive after a while, added good VR interactions, but made everything in a shorter campaign. That's basically this game. I've played with QGO (HD+ profile) and the graphics on Quest 3 are really good. Everything is super polished, gunplay is pretty good, and the atmosphere is great (one of the scariest games that I've played in VR). The only downside is that the game is short, but for its price it's a must-buy in my opinion.
Really enjoyed this one. Hopefully this dev makes more games
Yet another zombie fps game. The controls are clumsy and the graphics aren't great. Based on the reviews, I had hoped for something better.

reviews.review_title: Loved It! Hope For A Sequel
reviews.review_description: Such a fun experience! Always worrying if I have enough resources (ammo, batteries, health) as any good survival horror should make you do. Completely setup for a part 2 and hope the devs deliver.

I absolutely shat my pants playing this. The sounds, forever saying, "What's that?!" Doors creaking, the list goes on. At a very reasonable price too. I purchased for £8.99 absolute bargain. Thanks devs, you have put a hell of a lot of work in this game. Well done!!
Game is absolutely terrifying. It's concept is simple and the usual but the take it up to the next level especially with resources.

Let me start by saying, there aren’t many scary/ horror games on Oculus that I find immersive. I always end up refunding them. But if you want genuine, heart racing terror… THIS is your game. The atmosphere itself is just chilling, I’d probably still be terrified if there weren’t monsters hunting me down!😂

I’m not new to horror games or gaming in general either, I’m 22 and love QUALITY games, and this? this is freaking quality dude.

I was only able to play for an hour before putting it down because it is genuinely scary. This is the kind of game that you can only play in short bursts of bravery😅

There’s a sprint mechanic but I’m too scared to use it, the monsters might hear me😭 I also wanted to see if a wine glass was breakable if I threw it, but the game has me so on edge that I just set it back down quietly, so if you’re brave enough, let me know if it’s breakable😅

Don’t skip over this one, it’s hard to find games worth your time on this store, and I’m telling you, this one is worth your time. BUY IT!! The graphics are really good too, so if realism is your thing, you’ll love the way this looks.

You’ll be hooked instantly, it’s a phenomenal game so far.

This game really surprised me in many ways. I bought it during the spring sale and have about 2 hours of gameplay so far, and I can already say it was worth the purchase. I’m playing in fully immersive mode, and the atmosphere the game creates is exceptional. It feels a lot like playing Resident Evil 1, but in VR mode. The story seems simple, but it hooks you into the game because you want to know what happens next. The graphics are great for the Quest 3, and the gameplay is very smooth too. The game generates a constant sense of tension, which is a huge plus for horror games. I totally recommend this game, whether at full price or on discount. 5 STARS EASILY.
This is the best horror game I’ve played on the quest3 so far. Great graphics and a decent story it gave me a feeling like I was playing a resident evil game in vr.

This feels very polished, the visuals are great on quest3.
This is scary stuff, the atmosphere is almost overwhelming at times which made me take my first playthough in short stints, and even when I knew what was coming on my second play through, it would still make me jump.

The game is short, I like this especially for something so tense, my first game took around 3.45h but then a second game took 1.37h, I think if I really rushed I could even do it in around 1.15h, so yeah its short but very sweet, but if you explore everwhere and if you find-all of the collectables im sure it could run over 4 hours.
Recommended!

I like horror movies and games especially zombie. I had to take breaks from this game while I played it since it was stressful to play since it was pretty scary. I enjoyed it alot and look forward to part two. Please make a part 2 i will buy it.

I'm not one to be a scaredy cat, but this game has me taking breaks playing it because it scares me so much. 😂. I'm stuck on one level in the main hall with a guest book because I'm too afraid to go look for the key and also I keep running out of bullets trying to shoot these zombies and I'm too afraid to keep searching through different rooms 😭😭😭. The graphics and sound on this game are so good as well.

Hands down best horror game I ever played. This is a game that is expertly made! I haven’t felt this terrified since I first played resident evil as a kid. The developers need to make a part two ! Let’s make our way up that elevator!

Good game, but more story part two, 

we all want it.

reviews.review_title: The game is good but very scary
reviews.review_description: Good game but very scary, I can’t complete it

Played an hour and l already fell in love with it, will adjust my review when l complete the game but it’s everything a horror game should be. Jump scares, exploration, ammo and aid management. Really reminds me of Resident Evil 1 with creepy hallways, or the zombies waking up out of nowhere. What sets apart from Resident evil is its noise mechanic. Running, shooting attracts enemies. Making each action more meaningful. Most doors are locked so expect a ton of backtracking. I recommend this game to anyone looking to play a survival horror game

reviews.review_title: Were's the miss part of F4-7 and R？
reviews.review_description:
The game？yep，it‘s definitely remarkable，but the latest update for quest 3 is still need to be polished（like the hair‘s detail of zombies ）， I belive these new features and textures be added in is still far to reach the performance limited bar.

The horror vibe is mastered. especially you would be surprised by the cornor（if you are veteran you already know what's that mean）. For those zombies who are hide in darkness, well…I’m screaming and also enjoying.  Rating 4 out of 5

Were's the miss part of F4-7 and R？ Dev team

reviews.review_title: I have a REQUEST FOR META!
reviews.review_description:
I started this game, thrilled that there was a 2nd player, even though he was a computer, or part of the game. I'm big on horror movies along with games. But I also have one BIG setback.... my biggest fear are indeed zombies 😅 I know,  not a practical fear. So when I finally find zombie games or scary games that are either multi player, or for this games instance, has a second character. I'm thrilled!!!! But when I lost Owen.... my dude! My backup, my BODYGUARD! .... nope .... nope... NOPE!!!  I haven't been able to play since! But I'm hoping I'll find the strength to move on without Owen, and maybe find another character during the game in order to continue on my quest to find and save Emily's sister !🫣 Until then.... my request is, Please make more zombie or horror games with multiple characters!!! Or multi player! So yes, I know what you're thinking. 
"Well, we have (After the Fall) and (Arizona Sunshine) ok yeah they alriiiight🤷‍♀️ but I wasn't crapping my pants or anything. And the arcade style games aren't ever too scary. I do love BOTH of those games.
 5 stars 🌟  but I'm looking for something more intimate like this game and more characters like Owen. 😌  I'm sure a few can agree! THANK YOU!!! I LOVE YOU!! BYE!!!

It’s pitch black. It’s bananas. It’s pitch black bananas. Bring a flashlight, so you can see those gorgeous visuals. 

One of the top ten best games in VR.

If you are hesitated to buy it or not just go on if you like horror games ! 
A very nice experience the graphics is good the weapons and the story and the best thing is the jump scares you never know when some zombie is gonna jump at your face 🤣 so you must unholster your weapon and be ready to shoot!           Even if it is a rat 🐀 
Enjoy 😉

It is a great game, won't deny that, suspenseful, and some lovely emersion. 

However, this Dev has the habit of going "here have a game (4 hours of gameplay if you take your time) it is the first part of the story" then just abandon it. 

So maybe we will get the rest, I doubt it, though. Get it on offer, and never pay full price.

developer_response.body:
Hello He11swinter, 

Thanks a lot for playing Propagation: Paradise Hotel, we're really glad you liked it ! 

Although we are fully aware that the game's ending is a bit cleaving, we do not have "the habit" to offer first part of the story games and abandon them :(

Happy New Year
- Tiffany

reviews.review_description: This is easily one of the best games I have played on Quest 3. I’m hoping the reviews below 5 stars were from launch because I’m blown away by the quality of this game. It reminds me of the early Resident Evil games but in first person and on VR. If you are old enough to have played the first couple Resident Evil games,then you definitely know how creepy they are. This game keeps you on the edge of your seat. Every time you hear a screechy door,a random thud or a zombie moan,it gets extremely tense. Like the original Resident Evil games,you never know when the walking dead are going to pop out on you. If you aren’t prepared for some serious jump scares,this probably isn’t the game for you. I highly recommend this game to the true VR horror game fans

reviews.review_title: I got this the first day it came out
reviews.review_description:
I bought this game When it first came out the graphics sucked but had a good story but the graphics weren't enough to keep me immersed so only played it a little bit seen this update for quest 3 and its nice ..
looks good please good no complaints at all in fact this game is extremely underrated and should be at the top of the charts it's a must buy

Graphically, this looks like a great game to play, and partly it is. However, there are too many annoying design flaws in the game that either leave me feeling bored or tempt me to just move onto a different game.

These are:

1) The character needs HEIGHT adjustment settings like in the game "Into The Radius". I play this game in SITTING mode and find that the character's eye level is the same height as a door handle! And am fairly sure that the main character is not meant to be a dwarf. 

2) Once you kill a zombie in a certain location, it should REMAIN dead. But in this game the SAME zombie re-appears in the SAME location as you walk around the building. This means you need to keep wasting VERY LIMITED ammo on the SAME zombie! Targeting the Zombie is also tricky, so you waste even more ammo.

3) The method used to load the gun is OK when everything is quiet and you are not being attacked by zombies, but trying to do it when you are attacked is EXTREMELY difficult and fiddly. Often, you are dead before you get a chance to load the ammo. A MUCH swifter easier "one click" method needs to be available in settings to improve this.

4) Parts of the game leave you scratching your head. For example, not far from the start of the game you need to find an engineer named DAVID to get a key. You also need to find something to cut a chain. But I have tried EVERYTHING to do this without success. Obviously, I am missing something, but to make it SO hard to find is not good game design, it just leads to BOREDOM!

5) The game can be EXTREMELY dark in many areas, so you need the flashlight a lot or you cannot see anything or target a zombie. Unfortunately, you need to find batteries for the flashlight, which like ammo and health spray, is very limited! So most of the time you are wandering around in near complete darkness waiting to be killed by a zombie.

This game needs a LOT more settings and options. Plus more inventory items. Many games try to make things too difficult to make the game last, and I think this is one of them.
Really scary

EDIT: just finished the game and I have to admit that I really did enjoy it even though the ending kind of sucked. Waiting with baited breath for the next chapter. 

I prefer playing games which are slow and methodical (unlike games most people play nowadays). I also like horror games because of the adrenaline rush they give you. However I've yet to come across a game which has truly freaked me out. This game has come very close to that; something about the ideas of walking alone in abandoned hotel corridors, quickening your pace and your pulse at the slightest sound that you hear.  Yup...it's unnerving alright. 
Taking my time with this game and I'm enjoying it immensely. If you are looking for a truly horrifying game then I would pick this over other similar ones. Recommended.
To the developers I would like to suggest that they introduce a multiplayer option in which you have to look out for each other as well as lookout for your surroundings which would make the game a bit more interesting.
This game suck dongs

Pleeeease do not sleep on this game. I don't have the words to even describe this experience..it's gross,  it's gritty, it's down right diabolical and I'm in love with it. Forget every vr horror title...seriously they can't compare. If your thinking about it...do it. I didn't know what crapping bricks actually felt like until I played this game...not even on the  hardest difficulty and it still has me shook .I'm 28 years old and live off horror but not even I was expecting what this game had to offer. It may be on the shorter side but holy smokes is it a ride worth every second

It fills the survival zombie theme, the game is scary and tense, it's quite fun, good use of VR and can get hard.
Unfortunately the story is incomplete, there are also no extra modes and the game can be "finished" in 3 hours.

Recommended only on at least 25% sale as I hate when games are not finished.
Everything is great, story, graphics, gameplay, music, immersion. It's all there, great work, I enjoy this game very much.
The atmosphere of this game was f’n awesome. If you’re into horror games, this is THE ONE. Can’t wait for part 2 (fingers crossed it’s soon)

I got this on quest 2 when it came out now I have quest 3, why would I play half a game with quest 3 updates when the game was never finished. and no DLC for the game it's been uninstalled for over a year and it's staying that way, 

Well you telling us it's finished but ending on a cliffhanger should stop anyone with a brain not to buy it, if it's ended on a cliffhanger that proves there's no ending so it wasn't finished

developer_response.body:
Hey there, 
We're really sad to see that you think you wasted your money on our game. 
The game is finished. But ending with a cliffhanger is a very divisive end, we're aware of that. 
Hopefully, you decide to enjoy the Quest 3 enhancements at some point. 

 - Tiffany

reviews.review_description: Enjoying the immersive game, visually impressive and great for scares, the audio effects really add to the game play..  I have to play in the daytime as I get creeped out playing late at night…  The but…. is for the control and settings.  Running is a complete nightmare on the controls. At times you need to and I find the control intermittent, sometimes she runs but mostly doesn’t so I die, or if she starts running she stops and I cannot get her to run again, and then die.  I have asked around for support trying to understand what I am doing wrong but not had any advice back.  Also I seem to be too tall and struggle to reach down to open the drawers having to kneel.  There may be away to reset it but I cannot find it.

reviews.review_title: Super fun! but way too short…
reviews.review_description: Even at 16$(cdn) on sale I feel a little ripped off, the gameplay is S-Tier but the length is D-tier. I felt like the game was starting to get really good with the first big boss battle that was a nice challenge and got me really excited to see the next, only to be met with the games end credits. I was actually speechless lol It feels like the first episode of a series because the story didn’t conclude so I really hope they release an episode 2 as a free update, or maybe as a cheaper DLC but in no way should they be keeping this at 25$ if they’re going to be releasing the continuation as a full priced sequel.

reviews.review_title: A must buy for the quest 3
reviews.review_description:
This is a great game for any horror lover.
If the original resident evil and mark Zuckerberg had a baby it would be this!
The graphics are great, the atmosphere is terrifying and the sound is amazing. You are always on edge when you’re in a room trying to loot for your life and all you can hear is creepy sounds all around you. But it’s not all doom and gloom, Your best friend is a trusty flashlight…THAT ALWAYS RUNS OUT BATTERY!!! And trust me you don’t wanna be in darkness as it will give you a heart attack. This is a must buy bro!

reviews.review_title: Best (VR) Horror Game I've Ever Played.
reviews.review_description:
I'll do a bullet point list right off the bat and then we'll get into it..
• I have the Quest 2 and the graphics are more polished than Walking Dead & RE4 (VR)
• It's QUIET AF - the atmosphere & sound in the hotel is impeccable - WEAR HEADPHONES
• The monster AI is terrifying, sporadic, well-designed and FAST.
• The details to the story, the goals, the objective, it's solid & immersive.

Okay, I hope you're ready for a bit of a story. I grew up on Resident Evil for Playstation One. In my honest opinion, Resident Evil 2 (The original & the remake) is the best horror game ever made, besides the first Silent Hill. I've played RE7, RE4 and Walking Dead S+S, all in VR. Paradise Hotel is different. It's a fresh concept, all while maintaining what I believe is the best backdrop for a horror game, 'the haunted house'. There is music in the game, but for the most part, it's quiet, and WITH HEADPHONES on, haha (wear headphones) you'll really fall into it. I'm only like maybe 45 minutes in, I've hit one boss (he was TERRIFYING) I was actually scared, (I'm 40) and the surprises in the vents and the closets... just buy the game for $25.00. Play at night. Turn off your lights, draw the biggest boundary you can.. and wear headphones. 

For the developers, please tell me that you guys are working on something new (and longer). More weapons? (unless I haven't hit them yet) You're on really good path with this. Also, maybe a bit more interaction with random items as well? But hey you guys really hit a home run with this one. Resident Evil One homage. Seriously. Thank you for Propagation: Paradise Hotel.

The game is amazing, and the story is good. I can tell that there was a lot of attention to detail when they made this. It was very scary, immersive, and exciting! 

Before I purchased it I saw reviews about it being short. After playing the story and exploring most areas I would say it took me around 4-5 hours, which in my opinion is a bit short but it was 100% worth it! Although my only complaint was the ending and I wont state why because I don’t want to spoil it. 

Overall this game definitely earned 5 stars!

I've already played Propagation VR with PCVR and had the most intense experience with my quest in a horror game for the first time.  Propagation The Paradies Hotel is perfect for people who like zombie movies and like to explore a scary place with a flashlight and limited lifespan and look for a way out.  This game is definitely not for the faint of heart!
